AMS integrates with the Internet Banking Platform to provide account data and transaction services.
AMS integrates with the Mobile Banking App to support mobile account access and transactions.
AMS integrates with the Fraud Detection Engine to enable real-time transaction monitoring and fraud prevention.
AMS feeds account and transaction data to the Data Warehouse for analytics and reporting.
AMS relies on PostgreSQL as its primary relational database for storing account and transaction data.
AMS is deployed on and depends on AWS Cloud Infrastructure for hosting and scalability.
AMS performance and reliability are measured against a 99.9% System Availability SLA.
AMS data loss tolerance is measured by a 15-minute Recovery Point Objective.
AMS data persistence is subject to a daily encrypted backup schedule to S3.

context: Key Functions: - Account creation and lifecycle

description: AMS stores account holder relationships including primary, secondary, and joint structures.
context: Data Model: - Account Holders (primary, secondary, joint)

description: AMS processes and records transaction data for all managed accounts.

AMS tracks the current status of accounts throughout their lifecycle.

The Data Warehouse provides transactional data to the Transaction Mart for processing capacity monitoring.
The Data Warehouse provides product performance metrics to the Product Mart for scope tracking.

The Product Mart measures the scope of product performance data across tracked products.

The Data Warehouse's data lifecycle and storage operations are governed by the Data Retention Policy across hot, warm, and cold tiers.
context: Retention Policy: Hot data (3 months): Real-time - Warm data (1–3 years): Nightly batch - Cold data (3+ years): Archive (S3)

description: FCA COBS mandates data security and privacy standards to protect customer information
PRA Requirements mandate capital adequacy monitoring to ensure financial stability and solvency
PRA Requirements mandate operational resilience standards to maintain critical services during disruptions
PRA Requirements mandate cyber security standards as a core component of operational resilience
GDPR defines the requirement for data subject rights including explicit access and deletion mechanisms
GDPR defines the requirement for data protection by design to embed privacy into system architecture
GDPR defines the requirement for breach notification procedures to ensure timely regulatory reporting
The breach notification requirement is quantified by a strict 72-hour reporting service level agreement

CloudWatch provides performance metrics and alerts that trigger the incident escalation procedure.
New Relic tracks system performance and initiates escalation workflows when thresholds are breached.
CloudTrail maintains audit logs that are required as evidence during post-incident reviews.
GuardDuty detects security threats and provides findings that inform the post-incident review process.
CloudWatch and New Relic are integrated to provide a unified view of system performance monitoring.
CloudTrail and GuardDuty are integrated to consolidate security audit logs and threat detections.

The staff and contractors budget constitutes a major financial component of the overall annual technology budget.
The tools and licenses budget is a sub-category allocated within the total annual technology budget.
The training and development budget represents a specific allocation within the broader annual technology budget.
The technology contingency reserve is a reserved portion of the total annual technology budget for unforeseen expenses.
Cloud infrastructure (AWS) is a primary cost category funded by the annual technology budget.

The Organisation Model defines the overarching structure that contains and organises the Technology Departments.
context: [[organisation_model]] — Technology department structure / [[departments]] — Technology department details

description: Technology Departments are responsible for managing and maintaining the specifications of bank Products.
The Application Landscape integrates with the Logical Data Model to access and utilise structured data.
The Application Landscape relies on the Integration Specification to define connectivity standards and API contracts.
The Application Landscape is subject to the mandatory security controls and compliance requirements defined in the Information Security Policy.
The Disaster Recovery Plan is enforced by the Information Security Policy to ensure business continuity and data protection standards.
context: Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP-001) / Information Security Policy (SEC-POL-002)

description: The Logical Data Model serves as the foundational data source that feeds information to the Application Landscape.
